title: Komm Ssser Tod Asukas perspective of Instrumentality
display_title: Komm Ssser Tod Asukas perspective of Instrumentality
wikitext:
[]: Komm, Süsser Tod - Asuka's perspective of Instrumentality             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Tue Apr 18, 2023 9:44 am Years ago, shortly after finishing Evangelion for the first time, an idea popped into my mind. It's not something I'm sure of, but I believe it has some weight to it. In short, I noticed that despite being an integral part of the scene that kicked off Human Instrumentality, Asuka is nowhere to be found in the iconic Komm, Süsser Tod sequence. I find this to be rather intriguing as her presence can be felt (as evidenced by how she is associated with it by the fanbase). It was then that I started to realize that although she is not present in video, she leaves her mark in audio. No other character can be so personified by the lyrics of Komm, Süsser Tod as Asuka. For starters, the name of the song is in German and it's sung by a woman. But it goes beyond that, of course. The lyrics of Komm, Süsser Tod are all about the struggles that define Asuka's character. I know, I know I've let you down I've been a fool to myself I thought that I could live for no one else

[]: Re: Komm, Süsser Tod - Asuka's perspective of Instrumentality             Quote Post by C.T.1290 » Tue Apr 18, 2023 5:52 pm That’s a pretty interesting theory, I like that! And I think that it does suit her, and for the fact that it’s German. It makes me wish we would have seen an epilogue of some kind where Asuka has matured and tries to move on from her troubled past, and maybe possibly make things right with Shinji.

[]: Re: Komm, Süsser Tod - Asuka's perspective of Instrumentality             Quote Post by AsukaisLiterallyMe » Tue Apr 18, 2023 7:31 pm Am I remembering wrong or didn't she also attempt to slit her wrists and was saved by Nerv's security detail? After the mindfuck scene? It makes me wish we would have seen an epilogue of some kind where Asuka has matured and tries to move on from her troubled past, and maybe possibly make things right with Shinji.

[]: Re: Komm, Süsser Tod - Asuka's perspective of Instrumentality             Quote Post by DTGee » Fri Apr 21, 2023 12:42 pm I feel like the lyrics could also fit Rei too. It's kind of a major theme in Eva that all three of them are going through the same problems.

[]: Re: Komm, Süsser Tod - Asuka's perspective of Instrumentality             Quote Post by Asunji_Yuko » Mon May 22, 2023 1:47 pm AsukaisLiterallyMe wrote: Am I remembering wrong or didn't she also attempt to slit her wrists and was saved by Nerv's security detail? After the mindfuck scene? It makes me wish we would have seen an epilogue of some kind where Asuka has matured and tries to move on from her troubled past, and maybe possibly make things right with Shinji.
